nodes.label: Watchdog countdown event: fires when the counter reaches zero. On the first timeout it latches the interrupt and reloads the counter; on a second timeout (interrupt still pending) it asserts the reset signal when RESEN is enabled, then keeps reloading and counting.
Returns true while the device is locked. When locked, all register writes are ignored; the lock is cleared only by writing the magic unlock value 0x1ACCE551 to WDOGLOCK.

Backing store for the watchdog down-counter. Holds the count and models it lazily: the live value is derived from elapsed cycles and the clock divider only when read, instead of being decremented every cycle.
Resets the counter to its power-on value (0xFFFFFFFF).

Lazily computes the current count from the cycles elapsed since the last update, scaled by the clock divider. Returns 0 once the counter has underflowed, and the stored value while the timer is not running.
Folds the lazily-computed live value back into the stored counter.
Stores a new counter value (used on reload and when freezing the counter).
Returns the stored counter value without recomputing elapsed time.

Maps the WDOGCONTROL.STEP_VALUE field to the clock divider (1/2/4/8/16), defaulting to 1 for reserved encodings. The divider sets how many input clock cycles correspond to one decrement of the watchdog counter.

(Re)posts the countdown event. Cancels any pending event, then, when the timer is enabled (INTEN set and clock running), schedules the event for the number of cycles until the counter reaches zero (remaining count * divider).

Drives the WDOGINT output to match interrupt state: asserts when an interrupt is pending and INTEN is set, deasserts otherwise. Skipped in integration test mode, where WDOGITOP drives the signal directly.
Drives the WDOGRES output to match reset state: asserts when a reset is pending, deasserts otherwise. Skipped in integration test mode, where WDOGITOP drives the signal directly.

Register bank holding the device's runtime side-effects. The bank layout and reset values come from the WatchdogRegisters_temp template in wdt-registers.dml; this declaration adds the read/write behavior.
Load register: the value reloaded into the counter on each timeout and on interrupt clear. Writes are ignored while locked and do not reload the counter immediately unless INTEN transitions 0->1.
Value register (read-only): returns the live counter value via lazy evaluation rather than a continuously decremented field.

Control register: INTEN enables the watchdog and RESEN enables reset on a second timeout. Toggling INTEN reloads or freezes the counter and (re)schedules the countdown; STEP_VALUE selects the clock divider.
Interrupt clear register: any write clears the pending interrupt, lowers WDOGINT, reloads the counter from WDOGLOAD and reschedules the countdown.
Raw interrupt status register: bit 0 reflects the pending interrupt before masking by INTEN.
Masked interrupt status register: bit 0 is the raw status ANDed with INTEN, i.e. the interrupt as seen by the interrupt controller.
Lock register: writing the magic value 0x1ACCE551 unlocks the device; any other write locks it. Reads return 1 when locked, 0 when unlocked.

Integration test control register: enables integration test mode, in which the output signals are driven directly via WDOGITOP. Leaving test mode restores the normal interrupt and reset signal states.
Integration test output register: in test mode, directly drives the WDOGINT and WDOGRES output signals from bits [1] and [0] respectively.

Signal interface implementations. Ports model input signals into the device; connects model output signals the device drives. wclk is the watchdog clock input; because counting is modeled lazily, its edges need no handling.
USER-TODO: implement wclk signal handling here.
wclk_en input (clock enable): raising it resumes counting (refreshes the counter and reschedules the event when enabled); lowering it pauses the timer by cancelling the pending countdown event.
wrst_n input (active-low watchdog reset): a falling edge performs a full device reset; the deasserted (high) level is normal operation.
prst_n input (active-low power-on reset): a falling edge performs a full device reset; the deasserted (high) level is normal operation.
wdogint output: the watchdog interrupt line. Raises/lowers the connected signal with a NULL-object guard, so an unconnected interrupt is a no-op.
wdogres output: the watchdog reset line. Raises/lowers the connected signal with a NULL-object guard, so an unconnected reset is a no-op.
Performs a full watchdog reset: cancels pending events, clears the counter and interrupt/reset state, restores the key registers to their defaults, and lowers both output signals.
Device initialization: sets the counter and all state variables to their power-on defaults (device unlocked, clock assumed enabled, output signals low).
